metaTitle: Bed Bug Treatment NYC | Heat Treatment & Extermination | The Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bed bug elimination in NYC. Whole-room heat treatment + chemical options. Free inspection. 90-day guarantee. Licensed exterminators. Call now.
h1: Bed Bug Treatment in NYC — Heat Treatment That Actually Works
heroParagraph: Bed bugs are one of the most stressful pest problems a New Yorker can face — and one of the most misunderstood. DIY products don't work. Foggers don't work. Even many professional treatments fail without the right approach. Whole-room heat treatment is the gold standard: it raises the temperature above 120°F throughout the entire space, killing bed bugs and their eggs in every hiding spot — no chemicals, no residue, no exceptions. Backed by our 90-day guarantee.
bodyParagraphs: Bed bugs spread through luggage brought home from travel, used furniture purchased or found on the street, shared laundry facilities in apartment buildings, and through gaps in walls and floors between units. They feed exclusively on blood — human blood, ideally — and can survive up to 18 months without a meal. That means an 'empty' apartment you just moved into might not be as empty as you think.

The biology makes them exceptionally hard to eliminate. Bed bug eggs are resistant to most chemical treatments. Bed bugs in later life stages can detect repellent chemicals and avoid treated areas. This is why so many infestations survive amateur treatment attempts and grow worse. Heat is the only treatment that kills all life stages — including eggs — throughout the entire room simultaneously.
signs: Small rust-colored or dark brown bloodstains on sheets and pillowcases
Dark fecal spots — tiny dark dots — on the mattress, box spring, or headboard
Shed skins (exoskeletons) in mattress seams, bed frame joints, and baseboards
Live bed bugs — flat, apple-seed-sized, reddish-brown insects in mattress seams
Itchy bite marks in clusters or linear patterns on exposed skin
A sweet, musty odor in heavily infested rooms
treatmentApproach: We offer both heat treatment and chemical treatment depending on the infestation scope and property type. Whole-room heat treatment is our preferred method: specialized electric heaters raise the room temperature above 120°F and maintain it for several hours, killing bed bugs and eggs throughout every crack, crevice, and piece of furniture in the room. No preparation stripping required — beds stay made. No chemical residue. K-9 bed bug detection dogs are available for difficult-to-locate infestations. Post-treatment inspection confirms elimination before we close the job.

faqs.q: How do I know if I have bed bugs and not something else?
faqs.a: The clearest signs are the physical evidence: bloodstains on sheets, dark fecal spots on the mattress, and shed skins in mattress seams. Bites alone are unreliable — many people don't react to bed bug bites. Text us a photo of any evidence you've found and we'll give you a free assessment within minutes.

faqs.q: Do I have to throw away my mattress?
faqs.a: No. Heat treatment kills bed bugs and their eggs inside mattresses, box springs, and furniture without any need to discard them. Throwing away your mattress without proper treatment is usually counterproductive — bed bugs spread to walls and furniture and simply reinfest the new mattress.

faqs.q: How long does heat treatment take?
faqs.a: A typical apartment heat treatment takes 6–8 hours. During that time, you'll need to vacate the space along with any pets, plants, and heat-sensitive items (candles, aerosols, certain electronics). We'll provide a complete preparation list when you book.

faqs.q: Can bed bugs come back after heat treatment?
faqs.a: Heat treatment eliminates every bed bug and egg present in the treated space on the day of treatment. However, bed bugs can reinfest from adjacent units in multi-unit buildings. Our 90-day guarantee covers retreatment if bed bugs return within the guarantee period.

faqs.q: What does bed bug treatment cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Heat treatment starts at $300–$500 per room, or $1,000–$1,500 for a full apartment. Pricing depends on room count, unit size, and severity. We provide a written estimate after the free inspection. No money upfront.
